package: Goal 9: Finite-Dimensional OAQEC Intrinsic Observer-Algebra Tomography

scope.algebra_family: finite-dimensional represented C*-algebras direct-sum_i I_m_i tensor M_d_i
scope.minimality_scope: bounded by block dimension, number of blocks, and sorted algebra dimension order

core_claim: Full intrinsic product/* tomography determines finite-dimensional observer-algebra Wedderburn block type; weaker dimension/center/commutator-dimension shadows do not.

formal_definitions.observer_algebra: A_R is a finite-dimensional reconstructable *-subalgebra of the logical/code algebra.
formal_definitions.abstract_wedderburn_signature: A_R is isomorphic to direct-sum_alpha M_{d_alpha}.
formal_definitions.represented_wedderburn_signature: A_R is represented as direct-sum_alpha I_{m_alpha} tensor M_{d_alpha}.
formal_definitions.intrinsic_full_tomography_data: Region-local distinguishable code-preserving effects with product, adjoint, identity, trace/normalization, and commutator structure constants; no global logical labels.

theorem_style_claims.claim: The full product/* table determines the finite-dimensional *-algebra up to *-algebra isomorphism and hence determines abstract Wedderburn block sizes.
Adding channel fixed-point or commutant multiplicity data determines represented Wedderburn pairs (m_alpha,d_alpha).
For stabilizer regions, Goal 8's local Pauli response plus symplectic commutator tomography is the Pauli compression of the Goal 9 product/* theorem.

bounded_exhaustive_evidence.second_algebra: M_2 direct-sum M_2 direct-sum M_2 direct-sum M_2 direct-sum M_2

proof_obligations.claim: Regression check for the Goal 9 certificate.

known_vs_new.known_derived: The positive theorem uses standard finite-dimensional C*-algebra/Wedderburn structure and OAQEC observable-algebra language.
known_vs_new.new_programmatic_contribution: The package lifts Goal 8's observer-tomography question out of stabilizer labels, separates weak operational shadows from product/* completion data, and gives exact finite counterexamples for insufficient shadows.

expert_facing_interpretation: Goal 9 reframes observer algebra as an intrinsically tomographable finite-dimensional observable algebra. Weak shadows such as dimension, center size, and commutator dimension do not determine the algebra, but full region-local product/* response determines Wedderburn block type. Goal 8 is the stabilizer special case where the product/* data collapse to Pauli response plus symplectic commutator tomography.
limitations: Full product/* tomography is a strong operational input and is close to giving the algebra by definition. The next nontrivial frontier is determining which weaker recovery, relative-entropy, modular, or noisy response data recover the same Wedderburn type.
